title: onobrychis caput-galli var. brevispina
intro: Onobrychis caput-galli var. brevispina, also known as a variety of Sainfoin, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its distinctive flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Onobrychis caput-galli var. brevispina include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as being used as a natural remedy for treating fever and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is also used as a fodder crop for livestock due to its high nutritional value.
